entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Recommendations for SAP S/4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Hello @SivaNagiReddy,

in SAP Learning Hub there are quite a few resources that could be relevant for you.

Best starting point is Exploring End‑to‑End Implementation & Operations SAP Cloud ERP, Professional Services:

This learning journey contains a summary of relevant courses.

It also references Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as a prerequisitie which will give you a good basis for Public Cloud in general (if you haven't done this already).

As for certifications: Unfortunately I do not see any certification specifically for Professional Services in Public Cloud. Therefore, I would suggest starting with the broader certification for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant. 

In addition, you could also follow up with SAP Learning help center to see if any Professional Services-specific certification will become available soon.

For me personally, having certifications has proven valuable so far. As Public Cloud is a relatively new software, this is the best way to prove the current knowledge.

I hope this helps 🙂

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: SAC Connection
entry.content:
Getting error messages when attempting to create a new connection with S4.

Have followed this guide: Live Data Connection to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Edition via OAuth | SAP Help Portal 

Communication Arrangement and Communication User created with certificate uploaded to S4.

Error message is "We couldn't connect to your S/4 HANA system. For more information, see our troubleshooting page. Correlation ID: 2430844844294459b599315182723872"

I can confirm that the OAuth 2.0 Identity Provider User ID mapping mode is already set to 'User Name' and we are using the built in 'SAP_COM_0065' arrangement

Can confirm that the User attempting this has an account in the S4 system, usernames match, and has the 'BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST' business role assigned.

entry.title: KO020 "open down payments" blocks CLSD on investment WBS even though down payment is fully cleared
entry.content:
Title: KO020 "open down payments" blocks CLSD on investment WBS even though down payment is fully cleared — and do we actually need CLSD?

Hi experts,

Environment: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Company Code 1510 (JP), Scope Item 35F (Capital Projects).

I'm running an investment project (I.00001) with two WBS elements:

- WBS I.000011 (equipment procurement, ~JPY 2,000,000): PO with down payment terms → 2 down payment requests → 2 down payment payments → GR → supplier invoice with the 2 down payment requests applied on the "Down Payment Clearing" tab → automatic payment run covering the invoice + 2 down payment items together (3 line items).
- WBS I.000012 (equipment installation, ~JPY 500,000): supplier invoice without PO reference (direct cost posting).

For both WBS, periodic AuC settlement and final settlement to the final asset (20000006-0) are done. Final settlement via F3767 (processing type Automatic) ran with 0 errors; the asset is capitalized and depreciating. The down payment clearing is posted (down payment G/L account 12110000 balance = 0).

The problem: when I set the WBS to CLSD, I get

KO020 "There are still open down payments for WBS I.000011"

This happens ONLY on I.000011 (the WBS that had down payments). I.000012 has no issue. I've checked supplier open items and the down payment G/L balance — nothing open that I can find.

Two questions:

1. Has anyone seen KO020 persist after the down payment is fully cleared in Public Edition? Is there a residual down-payment record at WBS/order level (not visible in the standard clearing apps) that I need to deal with, and how?

2. More fundamentally — do we actually need to set CLSD at all? My understanding is that capitalization and depreciation work fine in TECO, and CLSD is only the financial-close status that's a prerequisite for archiving. If we just leave the project in TECO, is there any real downside (reporting, period-end, audit, etc.)? Or is CLSD genuinely required for an investment project that's been fully capitalized?

Thanks in advance for any pointers.

entry.title: Unable to activate Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM for Two tier SAP Landscape
entry.content: Unable to activate Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM for Two tier SAP Landscape

entry.title: Re: SAP RETAIL create allocation table reference to PO
entry.content: Shouldn't the scenario be other way round? A Normal vendor PO from vendor to DC, used as ref in Allocation table to "Push" flow through stock to Stores?

entry.title: Re: Artificial Intelligence & SAP EPPM
entry.content: Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: Questions and Answers about E-Invoicing and E-Reporting for France in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Dear Damien,

You will be able to activate an XML connector in the "Electronic Invoice for France" protocol within Document Settings to generate e-Invoices and e-Reports in UBL format. Users can export the XML files to a defined path and then use them on another approved platform.

Best regards,

Ingrid Macova

entry.title: How to activate Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Unable to activate Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM 

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: CTS - Assign Transports


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to activate Change Management in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Nice talking to you via Teams.

please kindly follow setup steps under guide: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/first-steps-to-work-with-sap-cloud-alm-deployment-scenario-for-sap-abap/ba-p/13627068

Any further inquires please kindly get back to us.

 

Best Regards,

Sunny

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite sharing within company group - how...
entry.content:
Hello,

to solve your requirements you have look at different topics

  • Custom Role Collections
  • iFlow User Roles
  • Access Policies
  • Service Instances / Services Keys to access iFlows
  • Transport Management
  • Message Monitoring & Cloud ALM
  • Audit Logs
  • Organizational processes
  • Project / Developer Onboarding
  • and more

I had a talk at DSAG Technologie Tage 2026 discussing the different topics in this regards.

If you have access to DSAG website you might find the presentation already. 

The presentation name: Enterprise Integration mit der SAP Integration Suite – Governance, Security und Architektur in hybriden Szenarien bei Würth

If you don't have access you can contact me.  I can share the presentation with you.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 08 - CXL case-when-then operator
Cloud ALM Custom application Crash
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We recently integrated a custom SAP BTP application into SAP Cloud ALM. Our requirement is to receive email alerts whenever the custom application crashes. According to SAP documentation, we need to use the Alert Notification Service. Could you clarify whether this Alert Notification Service should be subscribed to in the SAP Cloud ALM subaccount or in the subaccount where the custom application is running?"

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/health-monitoring/health-monitoring-setup-configuration/health-monitoring-for-sap-btp-cf.html

Note: Custom application and Cloud ALM is running in different subaccounts.

 

Regards,

MK

Does the latest public cloud version support internal orders?

Please help confirm, thanks!

Need to create a debit note with intercompany Process without delivery order.
required the financial Flow also

entry.title: Re: Possible issue in Alternative Account Determination a...
entry.content: Good day @6-Mar

entry.title: Difference between Scrap in Material Master/BOM and Operation Scrap in Routing
entry.content:
I am currently working on a PP (Production Planning) implementation and trying to deeply understand the system's behavior regarding scrap calculations in MRP.
Could someone please explain the precise differences and best practices for using the following three types of scrap in SAP S/4HANA?
Assembly Scrap / Component Scrap (defined in Material Master or BOM)
Operation Scrap (defined in Routing)
Specifically, I would like to know:
How does MRP handle each type of scrap when calculating dependent requirements? (Does it simply multiply the percentage, or does it calculate backwards sequentially based on operations?)
What is the recommended business scenario for using Routing-level scrap versus BOM-level scrap? (e.g., material-specific defects vs. process-specific defects)
Any insights, documentation links, or real-world examples would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: 銷售訂單出貨時可以跳過撿貨嗎?
entry.content:
SAP Machine Translation Portal translation:

You can skip picking when shipping sales orders?
Hello,

When a sales order is carried out for subsequent shipping, it is important to pick it up, and even if the delivery schedule can't be triggered, is it not set up for IMG?

Fiori ID: F0868

CB: CB9980000100

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: QC21 without usage decision
entry.content:
Finally (thanks Srini, our senior ABAP developer) we got the solution to the requirement mentioned in the subject !

Tcode BS02 to configure in the STATUS PROFILE used in our Inspection Types a USER STATUS (initial) with the corresponding Business Transaction (QM45) added with influence "Allowed". 

As a matter of fact now it is possible for every Inspection Lot with that Status Profile assigned to perform the QC21 even before the Usage Decision has been performed.

Hope this knowledge can help somebody else with the same requirement.

entry.title: Re: QC21 without usage decision
entry.content: Actually I cannot accept your solution but, in the meanwhile, we found a proper one by using the STATUS PROFILE with a specific USER STATUS with assigned the Business Transaction QM45 which is allowing to run the QC21 even for non UD ILOTS.

entry.title: SAP Business AI Platform partner-led adoption incentive
entry.content:
The demand for Business AI is growing fast, and partners are playing a key role in helping customers turn AI ambitions into measurable outcomes.

Join our upcoming webinar, SAP Business AI Platform partner-led adoption incentive, and learn how SAP is supporting partners through an incentive program designed to accelerate customer adoption of SAP Business AI Platform.

During this session, we'll walk through eligibility requirements, engagement package submissions, governance expectations, service delivery guidelines, and the incentive payment process. Think of it as a practical roadmap that helps you move from opportunity identification to successful project execution with confidence.

If you're involved in sales, presales, delivery, practice leadership, or partner management, this webinar will help you understand how to structure compliant engagements and maximize available incentives.

We're excited to help more partners bring Business AI value to customers while growing their own services business.

Register now. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014395.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessAI #PartnerSuccess #DigitalTransformation #SAPBusinessAI

entry.title: Re: In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, how can we restrict TECO...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please check this functionality:

Configuring Phase Control Codes

BAdI:EAM_PHASE_CONTROL_ACTIVATION can be used to automatically activate/deactivate the phase control code. 

Best Regards,

Nick

Thanks again for your help.

Best regards,

Álvaro

entry.title: Re: Portfolio Hierarchy creation Capabilities in SAP EPPM...
entry.content:
Project Portfolio Management capabilities are currently not available in S/4HANA public cloud edition, you may check an alternative partner solution to cover the portfolio management process.

UNISYS, S.L.U. | Integrated Portfolio and Investment Management for Cloud

entry.title: SAP BPC Dimension hierarchy processed when base member with data becomes parent node successfully
entry.content:
Hello

We have implemented a SAP BPC standard model in SAP BPC 2021. In one of the dimensions, we had a number of base level members which had transactional data. A user accidentally amended the hierarchy and made those members parent members in the Hierarchy PARENTH1. The system processed the dimension successfully without any error or warning and allowed us also to transport this dimension to the production environment.

This causes very serious issues in terms of displaying data because in reports we see data both in parent members without having data in the base level.

does anyone else faced the same issue?

entry.title: Unable to find sap business content SAP_HR_OWFP_Operational_Workforce_Planning

we have a question about SAP CPI.

  1. Can we set up CPI separated by client?
  2. How to see the actual use by client and what document are used the CPI by client

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Jira integration doesn't work for CALM event moni...
entry.content:
Hello Mina,
based on your description and the screenshots you attached, could you please change the resource type (screenshot : "JIRA Subscription.png") to Event Situation as you would like to set it up for Events - right now the screenshot shows it is set to issues.

If this does not solve your problem we would like to ask you to open a case in SAP Cloud ALM API component (SV-CLM-API).

Best regards,
Romy
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 08 - CXL case-when-then operator
NEW: 🇩🇪 SAP BTP Live Sessions auf Deutsch 🇩🇪
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

am Montag ging’s bei uns mit englischen Sessions los. Und heute legen wir nach. Denn Mittwoch ist doch eigentlich perfekt, um den eigenen Lernplan aufzustellen: Genug vom Wochenstart geschafft und genau der richtige Moment, um die nächsten Schritte zu planen 😉

Wenn ihr also Lust habt, eure Lernreise weiterzuführen, haben wir jetzt etwas Neues, speziell für unsere deutschsprachige Community:

👉 Unsere neuen deutschsprachigen Live Sessions starten Mitte Juli!

Freut euch auf verschiedene Themen rund um SAP BTP, ideal, um gezielt tiefer in einzelne Bereiche einzusteigen.

Hier ein erster Überblick:

🔹 Einführung in SAP BTP für Einsteiger

🔹 Einführung in die SAP Integration Suite für Einsteiger

🔹 Joule in ABAP (Durchführung in Deutsch)

(...)

Einige Sessions sind bereits offen zur Anmeldung, viele weitere folgen nach und nach. Es lohnt sich also, regelmäßig vorbeizuschauen. Wer weiß, vielleicht ist genau die nächste Session dabei, die perfekt zu eurem aktuellen Lernziel passt 😉

Sichert euch gerne schon jetzt euren Platz. Ich freue mich, viele von euch dort (wieder) zu sehen!

Viele Grüße,
Isabella

entry.title: Re: KO020 "open down payments" blocks CLSD on investment...
entry.content:
Read this KBA : 2130927 - Error KO020 while closing WBS element CJ20N for analysis/resolution.

  • Once you have verified that there are no outstanding open items, proceed with creating a support case

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 08 - CXL case-when-then operator
Create an own (Z*) Function-Module within an SAP-Owned Function-Group
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to create an own (Z*) Function-Module within an SAP-Owned Function-Group, Pros/Cons are not part of the Discussion.
The Function Group is M61Q.
I Created ZMD_BEARBEITEN_KOMPONENTEN as Copy of MD_BEARBEITEN_KOMPONENTEN
Within the INCLUDE LM61QUXX SAP add's automatically LM61QU20 as the ZMD_BEARBEITEN_KOMPONENTEN is the 20st Function-Module within the Function-Group.

Questions:
I was not asked for the access-key, so i think this is not a Modifikation? In the coding of ZMD_BEARBEITEN_KOMPONENTEN there's an "{ INSERT ...." Tag.
What is about changes by SAP within M61Q in the Future? Will ZMD_BEARBEITEN_KOMPONENTEN still be there after an Update? If SAP adds a new include LM61QU20 into the Group, will ZMD_BEARBEITEN_KOMPONENTEN then be in LM61QU21?

Many thanks

entry.title: Re: IS-U to S/4HANA utilities - Architecture in LeanIX

hi @Atul_Joshi85 thanks, I followed the guidelines for S4 and it is as you write currently. Just one question, by Scope Items you mean exactly what, please?
 If you have any sanitized example it would be really appreciated.
Thanks for your time and answer!

-Ivo

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025
entry.content:
Hi @Danny19 ,

Thanks for your response . I have got target host allowed by raising SR under: Allowlist Hyperscaler LB access. After which I was able to successfully register OTP . And at end required authorization was also assigned to development team in S/4 HANA. Still they are not able to view Joule in ADT. Is there any role we need to assign in BTP for developers or related to SR as it was raised under Allowlist Hyperscaler LB access?

entry.title: Re: CICD Actions for SAP Integration Suite / The Download → Git → Deploy pipeline
entry.content:
"When it is time to promote them, someone has to manually export the package, import it into the test system, apply the right configuration values, and deploy each artifact"

But if you import the artifact with the use of a transport you do not need to apply the configuration values, only the first time.
"If anything was already deployed in test, it might need to be cleaned up first."  It is also not necessary when you are import the change with transports.
Can you please elaborate why this solution is needed if there is also a good solution which is the transporting process?
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Tax code is not reflecting in work order
entry.content: You really need to be more specific

entry.title: Re: COPA Top-Down Distribution (TDD) Complex Scenario - MANAGE_ALLOCATION
entry.content:
Hi Hakan,

Thank you for your response. I believe the root cause is related to the RACCT field configuration in the TDD template.

In my CO-PA model (context: ACDOC_PA, model: ROYALTIES), the field RACCT (sender 622260)  is set to category "C – Retained and Relevant for Reference".

However, in the Manage Allocations app, when I configure the Reference Base Mapping for the Account Number, the field appears greyed out and cannot be modified in the TDD template.

The simulation returns:

• 12 sender records selected

• 0 result records generated

• Warning: Reference data not found

• Warning: There is no corresponding reference data mapped to sender (items 1, 2, 3...)

entry.title: Modernization of the OneOrder Save Sequence
entry.content:
Introduction 

If you work with service transactions such as service quotations, service orders, service contracts, and similar in SAP S/4HANA Service, the save sequence of the OneOrder framework is something you rely on every day, often without thinking about it. In the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release, this sequence has been fundamentally redesigned to align with the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming Model) transactional contract. 

This blog explains what changed, why it was necessary, and what the new phases mean in practice — especially the distinction between the introduced early save phase and the late save phase. If you maintain custom event handlers or BAdI implementations that rely on exchanging transaction IDs in the OneOrder save flow, this is directly relevant to you. 

  

Background: The OneOrder Save Sequence 

The OneOrder framework is the shared domain model underneath all service transactions in SAP S/4HANA Service. When a service transaction is saved, a central function module CRM_ORDER_SAVE orchestrates a sequence of steps: preparing data, validating the transaction, assigning ID from the number range, and finally persisting everything to the database. 

Historically, this sequence of steps was implemented as a small set of function modules with registered event callbacks and BAdIs: 

  • CRM_ORDER_SAVE_VALIDATE_OW: changes, validation, number assignment 
  • CRM_ORDER_SAVE_SAVE_OW: the actual persistence step including data exchange 

The framework events such as BEFORE_SAVE and SAVE allow partners and application teams to hook into this sequence. The problem was that over time, the BEFORE_SAVE phase had accumulated both data-modifying logic and pure validation logic in the same callbacks, with no clean separation between them. 

RAP to One Order before 2025OP FPS0

With the introduction of RAP, this mixed approach became incompatible from an architecture point of view. 

 

The RAP Save Sequence 

RAP enforces a strict separation of phases using Behavior Definitions (BDEFs). When a COMMIT WORK or COMMIT ENTITIES is issued, the RAP runtime processes the following save sequence: 

*The creation of follow-up transactions in the late save phase via CL_CRMS4_DATAEXCH_MGR=>PROCESS_FORWARD still uses older (B)APIs which rely on the source transaction ID. The ideal case would be to use CL_CRMS4_DATAEXCH_MGR=>PROCESS_FINALIZE and call the RAP Facade of the follow-up BO with a RAP-managed key exchange invoked by the CONVERT KEY statement. 

The distinction between early and late save is fundamental: The point of no return lies between CHECK BEFORE SAVE and ADJUST NUMBERS.  

Before that point, a save can still be interrupted — by returning errors from FINALIZE coming from BAdIs/Events/Actions after an erroneous data change, or by raising an exception in CHECK BEFORE SAVE due to a save check hitting the brake. After that point, the transaction will be committed. This is why the rules for each phase are so strict. 

 

FINALIZE 

FINALIZE runs in the early save before any irreversible action takes place. It’s the last opportunity to: 

  • Make changes to the source transaction 
  • Create follow-up business objects (BO) by their RAP Facade 

But it is also capable of interrupting the save: If FINALIZE raises errors, the commit is cancelled and the transaction remains open for correction. 

CHECK BEFORE SAVE 

CHECK BEFORE SAVE is the final gate in the early save before the point of no return. It runs after FINALIZE, meaning that all data is already in its final pre-save state. Here: 

  • All validation logic belongs here including checks for completeness, consistency, and business rules 
  • No data modification is permitted at this stage — neither via MODIFY ENTITIES, nor CRM_ORDER_MAINTAIN, nor direct SQL UPDATE. The RAP runtime enforces this strictly, and any violation results in a short dump. 
  • Errors returned at this stage will cancel the save, just like in FINALIZE. 

The key distinction: FINALIZE can still change things. CHECK BEFORE SAVE can only look at things. Both can stop the save. 

 

ADJUST NUMBERS 

Once CHECK BEFORE SAVE completes without errors, the point of no return is passed and the late save phases are reached. The transaction will be committed. 

The first late save phase ADJUST NUMBERS assigns the final transaction ID (from number ranges) for new transactions. Up to this point, objects use GUIDs. 

SAVE 

The last phase in the late save phase — SAVE — persists data to the database tables. This is also where legacy integrations, such as creating follow-up transactions via classic APIs, are triggered by the Data Exchange Manager (CL_CRMS4_DATAEXCH_MGR). 

 

Testing RAP Early Save Phases with COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE 

RAP offers a special variant for "dry-run" saves: COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE. 

This mode executes only FINALIZE and CHECK BEFORE SAVE, then stops. No numbers are assigned, and no data persisted. It allows callers to verify whether a transaction would pass validation without actually committing it—making it particularly useful for validation services and UI checks. 

 

The Problem: Lack of Early Save Phases in OneOrder 

Before the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release, the OneOrder save sequence had no concept of FINALIZE or a separate ADJUST NUMBERS phase. The entire pre-save logic — both data modifications and validations — lived inside CRM_ORDER_SAVE_VALIDATE_OW, which was mapped to the RAP CHECK BEFORE SAVE behavior definition. 

 This caused several concrete problems: 

  1. Data modifications in CHECK BEFORE SAVE caused dumps. 

CRM_ORDER_SAVE_VALIDATE_OW contained calls that modified data, including BAdI callbacks, event handlers, and internal updates. The RAP runtime detects such patterns and raises a short dump; consequently, any service transaction save triggering this logic in a RAP context would fail. 

  1. There was no FINALIZE equivalent in OneOrder. 

Logic that should have been executed in the FINALIZE phase — such as preparatory steps and last-minute derivations — had no dedicated place in the process. As a result, it was either omitted entirely or incorrectly implemented in CHECK BEFORE SAVE. 

  1. Number assignment was mixed into CHECK BEFORE SAVE. 

The number range assignment for service transactions was performed inside CRM_ORDER_SAVE_VALIDATE_OW. In RAP terms, this step belongs in the ADJUST NUMBERS phase rather than CHECK BEFORE SAVE. 

 4. Duplicate execution occurred in the SAVE BDEF. 

The SAVE BDEF called the full CRM_ORDER_SAVE Function Module, which internally called CRM_ORDER_SAVE_VALIDATE_OW again. As a result, validation logic ran twice, and in some cases, even the entire sequence ran multiple times. 

 

The Solution / New Architecture: Four Dedicated Classes 

With SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS0, the OneOrder save sequence has been redesigned to include four dedicated classes, each corresponding to exactly one RAP BDEF phase. 

RAP and One Order after 2025OP FPS0

Backward compatibility is preserved. The new classes are also invoked from non-RAP save paths, such as WebClient UI Framework, so existing integrations continue to work unchanged if they respect the phase contracts. 

One Order Save Sequence

What Changed for Event Handlers: New FINALIZE Event 

A new OneOrder FINALIZE event and a separate execution time 074 have been introduced. They are registered in transaction CRMV_EVENT. 

The existing BEFORE_SAVE event maps to CHECK BEFORE SAVE. Out of 78 standard callbacks registered for the BEFORE_SAVE event, nearly 2/3 were moved or split as part of this change because they contained data-modifying logic that does not belong in CHECK BEFORE SAVE. 

The rule for deciding where a callback belongs is as follows: 

  • Does it modify data? → FINALIZE  
  • Does it only check the condition and optionally raise an exception? → BEFORE_SAVE (CHECK BEFORE SAVE) 

  

Practical Guidances:  

Review Your Custom Callbacks 

If you have implemented custom event handlers in the OneOrder save sequence, it is important to review them now. We recommend proceeding as follows: 

Step 1. Identify your callbacks. Search for implementations of the BEFORE_SAVE event registered via transaction CRMV_EVENT or CRMV_EVENT_CUST View in IMG for the object types relevant to your scenario. 

Step 2. Classify each callback. For each function module, specify whether it changes data, verifies data, or does both tasks. 

Step 3. Register the callbacks under the appropriate event and execution time. If logic is moved to FINALIZE, re-register it under the FINALIZE event in CRMV_EVENT. Use execution time 074 for callbacks intended for the finalize phase. 

Step 4. Test using SIMULATION MODE. Use COMMIT ENTITIES IN SIMULATION MODE in a test report to exercise only FINALIZE + CHECK BEFORE SAVE without persisting data. This makes it straightforward to verify that your callbacks behave correctly in the early save phases. 

Review Your integrations that rely on transaction IDs 

If you have implemented custom integrations that rely on transaction IDs before they are assigned, you need to adapt. This affects new transactions that are saved for the first time and haven’t run through ADJUST_NUMBERS once but rely on the transaction ID in your custom BAdI-/PPF-/Event-supported integration. 

There is no simple solution to this. 

The cleanest approach aligned with the target architecture is to call a follow-up by their RAP-Facade and do the CONVERT KEY exchange in the late save. 

A more practical approach is to implement a pre-check in FINALIZE/CHECK BEFORE SAVE of the One Order early save and move the remaining logic into the late save, e.g.: into the Dataexchange-Manager. 

Affected Transaction Types 

The new FINALIZE propagation has been implemented for all RAP BOs in SAP S/4HANA Service like:

  • Service Order 
  • Service Contract 
  • Service Confirmation 
  • Service Request 
  • Service Order Template 
  • Subscription Order 
  • Subscription Contract 
  • Solution Quotation 
  • In-House Service 
  • Master Agreement 
  • Opportunity 
  • Activities 

 

Conclusion 

With the release of SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS0, SAP introduces early save phases that provide a clear alignment between the OneOrder save sequence and the RAP transactional contract. 

Changes in a Nutshell 

  • Four dedicated save-phase classes have been introduced. 
  • Legacy FMs have been replaced; each now maps directly to a single RAP BDEF. 
  • A new FINALIZE phase has been added to OneOrder. 
  • An updated event and execution time are now available in CRMV_EVENT, where data preparation and last-minute adjustments are handled. 
  • The CHECK BEFORE SAVE phase is now strictly read-only; any attempts to modify data will result in a short dump within the RAP context. 
  • Number-range assignment has been separated into its own ADJUST NUMBERS class, extracted from CHECK BEFORE SAVE. 
  • All BEFORE_SAVE event callbacks have been reviewed, with standard callbacks either relocated or split as appropriate. 
  • The point of no return is now clearly defined between CHECK BEFORE SAVE and ADJUST NUMBERS. 

Core Takeaway 

FINALIZE handles any last-minute modifications and can still interrupt a save. In contrast, CHECK BEFORE SAVE acts as the ultimate validation step, examining the fully prepared data to make the definitive decision about whether the transaction should proceed or stop before it's irreversible. 

 

Further Information 

  • ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model — Save Sequence  
  • ABAP Keyword: COMMIT ENTITIES

entry.title: Re: Material Type DIEN in public cloud
entry.content: Use SERV for your needs.

entry.title: Datasphere: Import objects with data included (LT) did not work properly.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are experiencing an issue while transporting objects between our SAP Datasphere tenants.

Source Tenant (Production)
Target Tenant (Quality)

Our objective is to transport Local Tables including their data from the Production tenant to the Quality tenant.

The process we followed is:

  1. Create an Export Job in the Production tenant including the required Local Tables.
  2. Enable the "Data Included" option during the export process.
  3. Execute the export and successfully generate the transport package.
  4. Import the package into the Quality tenant.

The issue is that, although the "Data Included" option is selected during the export, the Local Table definitions are imported correctly but the table data is not transferred to the ta

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation API - job error logs
entry.content: Please try POST workflow instance to trigger the workflow, jobID will be in the response, use this jobid to get the job status. https://api.sap.com/api/SPA_Workflow_Runtime/resource/ POST /v1/workflow-instances https://api.sap.com/api/SPA_Workflow_Runtime/resource/Workflow_Definitions GET /v1/workflow-definitions/{definitionId}

entry.title: Re: Requirement for Project-wise Customer and Supplier Li...
entry.content:
Hello @sivakumar_k571 

Just one more thought about this question.

Please note that in the example of the blog post  Custom fields for the "Manage Customer Line Items"... - SAP Community
the extension point FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION - Journal Entry Substitution is used.
This extension point is called after summarization and value changes are moved to Entry View and Ledger View.

However, there are other extension points like FIN_ACDOC_EXT_SUBSTITUTION - Journal Entry Item Extension Fields Substitution. At a first glance, this extension point looks similar but it is called before summarization and value changes are moved  to Ledger View only. There is update of values in Entry View.

Please double-check if you selected the correct extension point. You would need an extension point which updates both - Entry View and Ledger View.

Best regards
Steffen

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Trial – Booster and Subscription Errors with SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello @Yogananda 
Thanks for clarifying the deprecation. From what I see in my new trial lobby, I get “Not subscribed” when trying to create apps like before. Does this mean we cannot build the same type of apps we used to make in SAP Build Apps anymore? If the path forward is SAP Build, could you guide how to start building similar frontend apps there, or what workaround exists for trial accounts?

entry.title: Re: Difference between Scrap in Material Master/BOM and O...
entry.content:
Comparison of Scrap Types in SAP PP

 

Scrap TypeWhere DefinedHow MRP Handles ItBest Use Case
Assembly ScrapMaterial Master (MRP 1 view)Increases planned order quantity at the header level. If you need 100 finished units and expect 5% scrap, MRP plans 105.Finished/semi-finished product losses (e.g., defective assemblies, unavoidable yield loss).
Component ScrapBOM item or Material Master (MRP 4 view)Increases dependent requirements for that component. If 100 units are needed with 10% scrap, MRP plans 110.Material-specific defects (e.g., fragile parts, cutting losses).
Operation ScrapRouting (operation detail)MRP calculates backwards: if 100 units are required after an operation with 10% scrap, it plans 111 units before that operation.Process-specific losses (e.g., painting, heat treatment, machining).

entry.title: Re: Access to SAP Engagement Cloud hands-on practice system
entry.content: Hi @hanvivian0203 , thanks for signing of to SAP Learning Hub, student edition! I will take a look at this and see if I can get the system to be unlocked for you,

entry.title: Re: WebGUI Short Dump GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED in CL_HTTP_EXT_I...
entry.content:
Try this solution

https://me.sap.com/notes/2956503

entry.title: Residential demand flexibility
entry.content:
Why residential flexibility matters now — more than ever

The European power system is undergoing its fastest structural transformation in a century. Wind and solar capacity additions are accelerating. The share of variable renewable generation in Belgium, Germany, and the Netherlands already exceeds 40% of annual production and is rising sharply. This is broadly positive — but it creates a new problem: the grid increasingly needs demand to flex, not just supply.

At the same time, residential energy demand is itself becoming more volatile. The electrification of heat and transport — EU heat pump sales doubled between 2020 and 2024; EV registrations are on a similar curve — is concentrating large, coincident loads into the evening peak. The classic "duck curve" of solar overproduction in the middle of the day, followed by a steep demand ramp at dusk, is no longer a Californian curiosity. It is a Belgian, German, and Dutch operational reality.

RED III (Directive 2023/2413) is the third revision of the EU Renewable Energy Directive, part of the Fit for 55 package. Member States were required to transpose it into national law by 21 May 2025. Its compliance obligations span several dimensions that directly concern DSOs, TSOs and energy suppliers. EUR-Lex

Energy Communities — a legal right, not an option
Under RED III Article 22, renewable energy communities (RECs) are entitled to share energy generated by production units they own within their community, and to access all suitable energy markets — including balancing and ancillary services — on a non-discriminatory basis. MDPI
This means energy suppliers must be able to bill and settle community members separately from their individual consumption. The current billing infrastructure of most suppliers is not built for this.

EV & storage as active flexibility providers
RED III introduces rules that allow electric vehicles and batteries to provide flexibility to the energy system by channelling renewable electricity back into the grid when needed, and rules authorising consumers to produce their own electricity individually or within renewable energy communities without unjustified restrictions

For energy suppliers, RED III compliance requires:
Community energy accounting (who consumed what, from which source, at what time)
Prosumer billing (net metering / net billing differentiation)
Guarantees of Origin (GOs) tracking and reporting

 

What the journey looks like — four use cases, one platform

UC 1 — IDENTIFYUC 2 — ACTIVATE
Flexibility Profile & Energy Disaggregation AnalysisAnticipative Shift Recommendations
AI analyses 14+ months of quarter-hour smart meter data to detect appliances (EV, heat pump, washing machine), quantify shiftable volumes, and compute a household flexibility score — with 88–91% detection confidence. No customer survey. No hardware beyond the smart meter already installed.Day-ahead and intraday price signals, Elia grid signals, and PV forecasts are combined to generate personalised, appliance-level shift recommendations. Customers receive push notifications with a one-tap confirmation. Accepted actions write back to the supplier's portfolio management system in real time.
UC 3 — ENGAGEUC 4 — MONETISE
Gamification & Loyalty PointsAI-Powered Contract Recommendation
Each accepted shift earns loyalty points redeemable against bill credits, retail vouchers, or sustainability donations. Leaderboards, challenges, and level progression drive a 3–4x higher engagement rate than pure financial incentives alone. Churn among active participants drops measurably.Using the flexibility profile as input, an AI engine scores each customer against available tariff structures — flat rate, day/night, or dynamic flex — and recommends the optimal contract. Dynamic Flex customers save an average of €270/yr more than flat-rate equivalents. Supplier margin on the portfolio improves accordingly.

 

The solution portfolio that can make it real

SAP SOLUTIONROLE IN THE SCENARIOKEY CAPABILITY DEPLOYED
SAP DER — Meter & Market OperationsSmart meter data ingestion, measurement concept management, quarter-hour profile storageMeasurement concept instances per customer; AMI integration; 15-minute interval data at portfolio scale
SAP AI Core & AI LaunchpadFlexibility scoring; shift-window predictionML inference pipeline on smart meter time series; appliance fingerprint detection; confidence scoring
SAP BDCFlexibility portfolio reporting; segment analysis; KPI dashboards for operations and commercial teamsReal-time load curve visualisation; flexibility KPI aggregation; cross-segment benchmarking
SAP Customer Loyalty Management 
SAP-Customer-Data-Platform
SAP BTP custom gamification service 		gamification enginePersonalised action cards; one-tap shift acceptance; loyalty points ledger; challenge engine
SAP Emarsys Customer EngagementOmnichannel outreach — email, push, SMS — for flexibility campaigns and tariff migration journeysAI-driven send-time optimisation; segmentation by flexibility score; campaign performance attribution
SAP IS-U / S/4HANA UtilitiesDynamic tariff contract management; billing for time-of-use and dynamic tariffs; points redemption against invoiceTariff structure modelling; interval-based billing; loyalty credit posting to customer account
SAP BTP — Integration Suite & Event MeshEnd-to-end integration fabric; real-time event routing between meter, AI, engagement, and billing layersAPI-first integration; event-driven architecture for sub-second shift-acceptance write-back; scalable to millions of events/day
PARTNER ECOSYSTEM — EXTENDING THE SAP PLATFORM  
NET2GRIDEnergy disaggregation — appliance-level load detection from smart meter data without additional hardwareEnergy disaggregation engine; household appliance fingerprinting; flexibility score computation; integrates with SAP AI Core via BTP API
EnodeDevice connectivity & flexibility activation — direct API integration to EVs, heat pumps, and smart chargers for real-time controlOEM-agnostic EV & heat pump API; smart charging optimisation; device state read/write; flexibility dispatch to individual assets
Evolution EnergieTariff engine & dynamic pricing generation — ETRM-compliant tariff modelling and real-time dynamic price signal productionETRM framework compliance; price signal delivery to SAP IS-U billing and customer engagement layers
 
Want to learn more? Join our Espresso Session on June 16. The recording will also be available via the same link afterward.

entry.title: Re: COPA Top-Down Distribution (TDD) Complex Scenario - MANAGE_ALLOCATION
entry.content: My question: Is the fact that RACCT cannot be modified in the TDD template preventing SAP from matching the sender account 622260 with the CA4NET reference group? And if so, how can we make RACCT available/editable in the TDD template configuration?

entry.title: Activation for the B/S Reclassification Run for Master Data Changed
entry.content:
Starting with Public Cloud Release 2508.1, a new B/S Reclassification Run for Changed Master Data is available as part of the Advanced Valuation Runs.

To use this feature, you must first activate the Reversal Posting Logic for B/S Reclassification (by Maturity) by enabling the feature
FINS_FI_CLS_REC_REVERSAL (Feature ID: 106573).

With activating the feature, the new run will become visible in the system (Configuration Activities as well as a job in the app Schedule General Ledger Jobs). 

There is no dedicated feature for the run itself that need to be activated.

what is Root cause of this message can you please explain.

regards

sanjay jadhav

entry.title: Re: SAP EAM Fiori Skip Dispatch phase without Resource Sc...

entry.title: Re: Create an own (Z*) Function-Module within an SAP-Owne...
entry.content:
NEVER copy a standard function module, do a modification of the standard because you'll be informed by the upgrade process (SPAU). It has been discussed a lot in the forum in the past years, and the old way (copying standard) is now considered completely wrong.

NB: S/4HANA doesn't request access keys anymore, but anyway modifying anything standard is a modification of standard.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Release: Streamlining Data after Employment Terminations in SuccessFactors Employee Cent
entry.content:
Hi @sahir_nidagundi,

Thanks for sharing this! You’re absolutely right, this issue was known and has since been resolved. A patch with a fix was deployed with 1H2026P7, and the update has already been rolled out to both preview and production environments as of last week.

The issue should therefore be resolved. If you’re still experiencing any problems or have further feedback, please let us know, we’d really appreciate your input.

entry.title: Requirement of exploring CBP in IBP

entry.title: From “AI in Applications” to “AI on Applications”
entry.content:
If you work hands‑on with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), you’ve likely noticed that AI strategy announcements and new platforms often introduce ambitious ideas and evolving narratives. It’s common to look for clear connections to how these changes influence modeling, governance, integration, and consumption of data in practice. With SAP’s Autonomous Enterprise strategy and the announcement of the SAP Business AI Platform (BAIP) at Sapphire 2026, many data professionals are asking whether this represents a real architectural shift or simply a reframing of existing capabilities.

This post is written for practitioners already familiar with SAP Datasphere, SAP BW, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP HANA Cloud, and SAP Master Data Governance, and focuses on what this strategy means in technical and operational terms for data professionals working with BDC today. It also offers a completely new perspective to data professionals who have never worked with these technologies and are intrigued by SAP’s Autonomous Enterprise vision.

What is not changing

Before discussing what’s new, it’s important to establish what remains true.

The Autonomous Enterprise doesn’t imply unattended automation or a loss of human supervision. AI agents operate within boundaries defined by data models, semantics, authorizations, and governance policies. Human intent, stewardship, and architectural decisions remain foundational. BDC is also not being replaced. BAIP doesn’t sit alongside BDC as a competing data platform. It builds on BDC and depends on it. Without harmonized data, preserved business context, and governed semantics, the Autonomous Enterprise cannot function reliably.

If anything, this strategy reinforces that strong data foundations are not optional for AI, they are prerequisites.

Why SAP is changing the AI engagement model

Most enterprise AI to date has been embedded inside applications. Predictive scores, recommendations, or copilots are scoped to a single process or system, often with limited visibility across business domains. The Autonomous Enterprise introduces a different model: AI on applications. Instead of embedding AI inside each application, SAP is introducing an agent‑based engagement layer that operates across applications, workflows, and data domains. These agents can reason over business intent, traverse processes, and execute actions using governed enterprise data. This shift only works if AI can reliably understand business meaning at scale. That requirement is what makes BAIP and BDC inseparable.

BAIP from a data architecture perspective

From a technical standpoint, SAP Business AI Platform is not a single service or runtime. It is a composition of cloud services that bring together AI models, agents, orchestration, governance, and data access into a coherent platform. Within BAIP, BDC makes the core of “Contextualize and Reason” services along with SAP Knowledge Graph. Its role is to provide AI agents with access to data that already carries business semantics, lineage, and policy enforcement. This includes SAP‑managed data products, customer‑defined derived data products, and harmonized data across SAP and non‑SAP sources using zero‑copy and federated data access patterns.

With a deep business process expertise that no one can match, SAP systems contain the most critical business data, forming the center of gravity for data and AI applications. Just to illustrate the size of SAP’s metadata model, SAP Knowledge Graph for SAP S/4HANA alone is based on 452,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views, and 7.3 million fields. That said, AI agents do not reason over raw tables or loosely defined datasets. They reason over data products with defined meaning, relationships, and access rules. BDC is the system that supplies those constructs.

 SAP Business AI Platform

How this addresses real data practitioner challenges

For data practitioners, the relevance of the Autonomous Enterprise strategy becomes clear when mapped to long‑standing pain points. 

Data context loss across systems remains one of the most expensive problems in enterprise data landscapes. Even modern architectures often strip business meaning as data moves between systems. In the Autonomous Enterprise model, AI agents rely on BDC’s business data fabric to preserve object relationships, hierarchies, and semantics across SAP and non‑SAP environments.

Semantic inconsistency is another persistent issue. KPIs and dimensions drift as data is replicated and remodeled. Under BAIP, semantic definitions authored in BDC – through Datasphere models, BW objects, HANA calculation views, and governed master data – are reused not only for analytics, but also for AI‑driven reasoning and action.

Manual orchestration and governance overhead is the third major friction point. In the Autonomous Enterprise architecture, governance is enforced by design. AI agents discover and consume data through BDC services that already apply authorization policies, data product contracts, and stewardship rules. Governance becomes intrinsic to execution rather than an after‑the‑fact control.

How this maps to BDC components

At a practical level, the Autonomous Enterprise strategy doesn’t introduce a new data stack. It activates existing BDC components in a broader execution context.

  • SAP Datasphere continues to provide semantic modeling, data product definition, and cross‑system harmonization. Those models are no longer consumed only by dashboards and planners, but also by AI agents operating through BAIP.
  • SAP BW remains a governed source of enterprise semantics and historical business logic, with its models surfaced as data products that retain their meaning when consumed by analytics, planning, or AI.
  • SAP HANA Cloud contributes intelligent compute, advanced SQL, and increasingly AI‑aware capabilities that support both transactional and analytical data products used by agents and applications.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud remains the primary analytical and planning consumption layer, while also becoming a beneficiary of AI‑assisted insight generation grounded in the same governed data products.
  • SAP Master Data Governance provides the quality and consistency layer that ensures AI agents reason over accurate, harmonized master data rather than fragmented representations.

Together, these components form the trusted knowledge core that BAIP relies on to make agent‑based AI reliable and explainable.

The technical takeaway

The Autonomous Enterprise strategy doesn’t reduce the importance of data engineering, modeling, or governance. It raises the bar. Putting AI on applications only works when AI can rely on consistent semantics, preserved business context, and enforced governance. BDC provides that foundation. BAIP operationalizes it. For data professionals, this strategy is less about adopting a new platform and more about extending existing BDC practices into an AI‑first execution model.

Join the conversation

These changes will surface new architectural patterns, design trade‑offs, and best practices. The Autonomous Enterprise will not be built in a single release. It will be built incrementally on the data foundations practitioners are already creating today. Join the Data Professionals community discussions to exchange technical perspectives, share lessons learned, and explore how others are evolving their BDC foundations to support agent‑based AI.

entry.title: MyAccess supports SCIM APIs
entry.content: We want to setup a Database (MyAccess) as the source system for IAS user store using IPS. Client wants to use an API to do send the data from MyAccess to CIS user store. Is this feasible and if yes, could you please guide us with the process to do this.

entry.title: Re: LIST_TO_ASCI no longer works with ABAPLIST in EHP7
entry.content:
DON'T pass a variable TYPE TABLE OF STRING to the parameter LISTASCI because it will do a short dump.

Use a table with lines of type flat character (C). Example:

TYPES: BEGIN OF vlist,
      line TYPE c LENGTH 1024,
      END OF vlist.
TYPES vlist_tab TYPE TABLE OF vlist.

DATA lt_listobject TYPE table_abaplist.
DATA lt_listasci   TYPE vlist_tab.

...

PERFORM list_to_asci 
      USING    lt_listobject
      CHANGING lt_listasci.

FORM list_to_asci
      USING    it_listobject TYPE table_abaplist
      CHANGING et_listasci   TYPE vlist_tab.
  REFRESH et_listasci.
  CALL FUNCTION 'LIST_TO_ASCI'
    TABLES
      listasci   = et_listasci
      listobject = it_listobject
    EXCEPTIONS
      others = 3.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: Re: Horizon Theme and Templates for Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @sebastianchindris , @mdelibasic ,

I forwarded your feedback to the development team.
My team currently does not plan to further update the Theme provided on GitHub because there is a roadmap item that is planned to replace the Theme that my team provided on GitHub:
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=67838200100800006884&q=Theme&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=C8287371FFCE44529E0534E565341211

Best regards,
Felix

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge!
Re: Job information portlet
entry.content: Hi @shynu92 , regarding Division field no data, it might be due to improper association configuration. Please navigate to Check Tool > Area: Association and run the three checks related to association. Regarding Department field being not visible, pls check if necessary permission is granted and if there's a business rule setting the visibility of this field.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud Label printing with templates stored...
entry.content: Hi Chris, those options helped a lot thank you. Best regards Luca

entry.title: Re: Horizon Theme and Templates for Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Thanks for the feedback @felixjonathanmueller !

entry.title: Re: ALM Google Chat implementation - Method doesn't allow...
entry.content:
Hi Tim,
from SAP Cloud ALM side we might not be able to help you with this as we are just inheriting the destinations from BTP.
Hence did you already check the BTP Destination documentation? 

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/http-destinations with regards to property settings of the destinations where you could for example set a property for API Key. 
If you need more assistance on that you might need to reach out to our colleagues from BTP side. 
Thanks and best regards,
Romy
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Teaching AI Agents Best Practices: A Skills Workspace for CAP Development
entry.content:
Hi,

we are also using CAP Java Developement supported by AI driven development by github copilot in Intellij. In the last few months i setup a small set of instructions where the normal ai models failing with cap framework like this:  

- You MUST search for CDS definitions, like entities, fields and services (which include HTTP endpoints) with cds-mcp, only if it fails you MAY read \*.cds files in the project.
- You MUST search for CAP docs with cds-mcp EVERY TIME you create, modify CDS models or when using APIs or the `cds` CLI from CAP. Do NOT propose, suggest or make any changes without first checking it.
- in every change or creation of java files, you must verify that the code compiles without errors.

With new models like Opus the gap between unterstanding the cap framework decreases. But i also see that the most answers i getting from ai refering to cap - js . I guess most training data is from there.

 

@daniel_schlachter  is it possible for sap to provide your instructions, skill files in general or in "closed" way ?  Maybe also as example like "cloud-cap-java-samples" project in github.

Btw. with the latest Github Copilot Plugin version for Intellij the folder structure was changed. I have to rearrange it.

entry.title: Re: Create an own (Z*) Function-Module within an SAP-Owne...
entry.content:
Thank you very much, I have also an R/3 System here and testwise I tryed to copy the FM there, I was asked for an Access-Key. I will set up a meeting with the customer and will gofor the modifikation withing the SAP-FM.

Actually I have been set on the wrong track when I copied the FM in S/4 and not beeing asked for the access key by falsely thinking copying is not not a modification.

According to the Mandalorian: This is the way!

entry.title: Re: Update SAP Market Communication
entry.content:
Dear Holger,

Please, just one question regarding licenses.

When we are operating the S4HANA for Utilities onPrem solution with a standard license and we would like to use APE only (without MPM) for our custom processes support, is there any separate license necessary? E.g., APE is related to MPM in licensing point of view etc.

Could you clarify this, please?

Many thanks!

With regards,
Ondrej

Since our client would like to use "AND" condition to narrow down the filter result.

Best regards,
Jean

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - Unable to Add New Process Input Variables (Add Button Disabled)

entry.title: Analytical Query Inquiry
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I have a concern regarding the result I am getting.

First, I joined PurchaseOrderItem (POForStoreOrderSummary) with MaterialDocumentItem2.



When I check the data using Customer Data Browser, I get the following results:

  • Goods Movement Type 643 = 10 quantity
  • Goods Movement Type 101 = 4 × 1 = 4 quantity

 

In the Analytical Query, I applied the following filters:

  • Goods Issue = 643
  • Goods Receipt = 101

However, I am having an issue with the result because the Goods Issue quantity is showing as 14.



Could you please help me understand why the Goods Issue quantity includes the quantity from Goods Movement Type 101?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Visualize Your Application Vulnerabilities in the BTP Cockpit
entry.content:
Hi @Fabian_Richter 

I have a couple of questions and it would be great if you could answer them -

1. What is the frequency of the scan ? I have subscribed to the service today , when can I expect to see the results of the scan ?

2. I have subscribed to the instance in two different subaccounts but to the UI in only one subacccount . Will I be able to see reults from both subaccounts in one place ?

3. Is there any way I can run the scans on request rather than on an SAP decided schedule ?

Thanks ,

Himanshu

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 08 - CXL case-when-then operator
Re: Depreciation keys for the assets
entry.content:
Hi Mattia,

Please follow the instructions on the SAP Help Portal Depreciation Keys. SAP Pre-delivers most of the depreciation keys needed in the activated country. Nevertheless, if you miss some, you can ask SAP for their creation through CASE.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: Create a Welcome email when a new employee is hired w...
entry.content: @jo_styles-01 , you might be able to handle this via SF Alerts, to be triggered one day after the Hire Date and a specific Alert Message generated via Document Generation.

entry.title: Re: Issues with Build Process Automation project becoming unresponsive
entry.content:
did you open the 2 projects with the same browser? if you open the 2 diff projects in diff browsers, will this issue happen?

if you upgrade and restart the agent, will this happen?

entry.title: Re: How to find authorization roles with an object and specific field values tied together?
entry.content:

thank you very much for your reply. 

Finding the user after I found the roles I'm looking for is not a problem. The problem is finding the exact roles with the required combinations of field values. Unfortunately I could not archive that with SQVI as I could not join the same table.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio – Cannot reliably access process outputs via prepared_result.prepared_mappings
entry.content: I would suggest to create a sap case under Joule Studio component CA-BLD-CORE-AG

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_schad ,

Our company is looking for formal clarification regarding the migration, data safety, lifecycle support, and licensing requirements for our application project following the retirement announcement of standalone SAP Build Apps.

Our Current Status:
 1. We have exported our frontend web project backup file (.gpg) from the standalone SAP Build Apps environment.
 2. We created a new SAP Build Free Trial account.
 3. We successfully imported the .gpg file into the new SAP Build lobby. The project currently loads, and we can view it normally within the low-code app editor.

While we see the warning stating that the standalone Free and Community Editions will be fully deprecated on 30 September 2026, we do not see an explicit deprecation warning inside the unified SAP Build trial lobby interface where our project now resides.

Could we please get formal clarification on the following four points:
 1. Validation of Migration Steps: Is the manual export and import of the .gpg file into the unified SAP Build lobby all that is required to fully safeguard our frontend project configuration from the upcoming September 30 deadline?
 2. Editor Support Lifecycle: Will the low-code visual drag-and-drop editor interface (and web target platform deployment) continue to be fully supported within the unified SAP Build platform moving forward, or is the low-code frontend format being phased out entirely across all subscription levels, and if so when, and what steps will we need to undertake?
 3. Requirement for Pro-Code Transformation: SAP’s official documentation ("SAP Build Apps – The Path Forward") references a transformation path into React or SAPUI5 code for use in SAP Business Application Studio. Given that our project successfully imported as a .gpg file into the new lobby, are we still required to request code conversion to ensure the longevity of our application?
4. Subscription and Licensing Requirements: To move this project out of the Free Trial and into a secure, productive environment, what specific tier of subscription is required? Will the standard paid SAP Build subscriptions continue to host and execute these low-code runtime projects after September 30, and if so for how long?

Thank you for your guidance on this transition. I look forward to your response so we can align our commercial and development strategy accordingly.

Best regards,

Louis

entry.title: Re: How to Debug SAP CPI Groovy Scripts Locally Using Visual Studio Code.
entry.content:
Hello @maria_orellana, I´ve switched over to Groovy IDE. Alternatively you can use a Template for IntelliJ from github 

GitHub - karthikmb86/CPIGroovyDebugger: SAP CPI Debugger for ease of Debugging · GitHub

entry.title: TypeScript 6 and 7 – What UI5 TypeScript Developers Need to Know in 2026
entry.content:
TL;DR

What you useTS 5.xTS 6.xTS 7.x
UI5 type definitions in UI5 namespace  (@openui5/types, @sapui5/types)worksworks, no changes neededworks, no changes needed
UI5 type definitions in TS default namespace  (@types/openui5)works, but deprecated — switch to @openui5/typesworks, but deprecated — switch to @openui5/typesworks, but deprecated — switch to @openui5/types
@ui5/dts-generator (if you generate types for your own library)works (now requires Node ≥ 20)works (now requires Node ≥ 20)works (now requires Node ≥ 20)
@ui5/ts-interface-generator (custom control interface generation)works (TS ≥ 5.2)works, no changes needednot supported right now — peer dep capped at <7.0.0

This table means:

  • If you're staying on TypeScript 5.x, nothing changes — except that you should switch from @types/openui5 to @openui5/types at some time in 2026 at your convenience.
  • If you're upgrading to TypeScript 6.x, also nothing changes from UI5 perspective: the UI5 types and tooling work fine. But you'll need to deal with the general TS6 tsconfig migration that every TypeScript project faces.
  • TypeScript 7.x is not supported by @ui5/ts-interface-generator right now, but otherwise everything behaves like TS6.

Independently, in case you still use @sapui5/ts-types-esm or @openui5/ts-types-esm, replace with the identical @sapui5/typesor @openui5/types as the former are no longer released now.

What's Happening in the TypeScript World

TypeScript is going through a significant transition:

TypeScript 6 (released March 2026) is the last JavaScript-based TypeScript compiler. It's a bridge release: settings that were deprecated with a gentle warning before now cause hard errors. You can silence them with "ignoreDeprecations": "6.0" in your tsconfig — but the message is clear: adapt now, because these settings will truly disappear in TS7.

TypeScript 7 is a complete rewrite of the compiler in Go. The headline number: roughly 10x faster compilation. The catch: the entire JavaScript-based compiler API — ts.createProgram, ts.factory, ts.TypeChecker, everything that external tooling like ours builds upon — simply does not exist (at least not yet). There is no programmatic API for tools yet. The TypeScript team has indicated this will come in later 7.x releases, but there is no timeline.

TypeScript 6 will be maintained "until TS7 reaches sufficient maturity," making it the thing to use for the foreseeable future.

The TS6 Changes That Affect Your tsconfig

If you're planning to upgrade your project to TypeScript 6, here are the settings that now cause hard errors:

 

Setting

What happens in TS6

moduleResolution: "node"

Hard error. Recommended replacement: "bundler" or "node16"/"nodenext"

module: "commonjs" (without explicit moduleResolution)May resolve differently
No types field in tsconfigNow defaults to [] — no more auto-discovery of @types/* packages

All of this is a general TypeScript 6 migration concern. Every TypeScript project faces it, regardless of whether it uses UI5 or not. But that last point — the end of auto-discovery — has a specific consequence for us.

Deprecating @types/openui5 in Favor of @openui5/types

You might be wondering why @types/openui5 exists alongside @openui5/types in the first place. The answer: convenience. Packages under the @types/ namespace on npm are special in TypeScript — they are automatically discovered and included without any configuration. Install @types/openui5, and TypeScript just finds the UI5 type declarations. No tsconfig changes, no explicit references.

With TypeScript 6, that convenience disappears. The types field now defaults to [], meaning nothing is auto-discovered — not even @types/* packages. You have to explicitly list every type package you need. And once you have to explicitly configure it anyway, there is no longer a reason to prefer @types/openui5 over the basically identical @openui5/types. Plus, the release process of the former via DefinitelyTyped, as they own that prefix has been... complicated.

We are therefore deprecating @types/openui5. The package will continue to be published for at least the remainder of 2026, so there is no immediate rush. But we encourage you to switch to @openui5/types soon at your convenience — it's the package we maintain directly and will be the sole one going forward.

How to switch

The migration is straightforward:

1. Replace the dependency in your package.json:

npm uninstall @types/openui5
npm install --save-dev @openui5/types

2. Update your tsconfig.json:

If you previously had @types/openui5 working via auto-discovery (no types field in your tsconfig), or if you had "types": ["openui5"], change to:

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    ...
    "types": ["@openui5/types"]
  }
}

That's it. The type declarations are practically identical. Your code doesn't change, only the package name and the tsconfig entry.

Note: If you're on TypeScript 5.x, you don't strictly need to change right now. But once you upgrade to TS6 or you want to consume newer versions of the UI5 type definitions beyond 2026, you'll need to make this change anyway — so it's a good idea to do it proactively.

Using UI5 Type Definitions

Types-related package switch aside, here's the good news: the type definitions themselves are completely unaffected by the TS6/7 transition.

Why? Because the types use ambient module declarations:

declare module "sap/m/Button" {
  // ...
}

This is fundamental TypeScript syntax, stable since TypeScript 2, resolved by simple string matching. It doesn't depend on module resolution strategy, compiler architecture, or any deprecated feature. It just continues to work.

So if you're upgrading to TS6 or beyond, you don't need new types, you don't need to regenerate anything. What you will need to do is adjust your tsconfig.json, because TypeScript itself now rejects some settings:

  • Replace "moduleResolution": "node" — we recommend "bundler" as it works without requiring file extensions on relative imports and is available since TS 5.0. Apart from that, "node16" and "nodenext" works as well.
  • Add a types field listing the packages you need (see section above).

But again: these are steps you'd take for any TS6 upgrade. Nothing UI5-specific here.

Using @ui5/dts-generator

If you generate type definitions for your own UI5 library (rather than consuming the ready-made ones we publish), you're using the dts-generator. It bundles its own TypeScript internally — it is completely isolated from whatever TypeScript version lives in your project and will continue to use its internal TS6 dependency even if your project uses TS7. The generated output is version-agnostic: same ambient declarations, regardless of whether the generator runs TS5 or TS6 under the hood.

What did change: We dropped support for Node.js 16 and 18. If you run the dts-generator in your own build pipeline, you now need Node.js 20 or higher. Given that Node 18 reached end-of-life more than a year ago, this should not come as a surprise.

Using @ui5/ts-interface-generator

This is the tool most affected by the TypeScript transition: the interface generator is deeply integrated with the TypeScript compiler API. It constructs AST nodes via ts.factory, observes file changes through ts.createWatchProgram, and resolves types using ts.TypeChecker etc. These APIs still exist in TypeScript 6, but they do not exist in TypeScript 7.

On TypeScript 5.x: Nothing changes.

On TypeScript 6: Same — fully supported. We verified that all compiler API methods the tool uses are present and functionally identical in TS6.

On TypeScript 7: Explicitly not supported. We've capped the peer dependency at <7.0.0. Once TypeScript 7 publishes to npm as typescript@7.x, you'll receive a clear peer dependency warning.

What's our plan for TS7?

  • Medium-term: We're evaluating bundling our own TypeScript (similar to how the dts-generator does it), which would decouple the tool from your project's TS version entirely but come with some drawbacks.
  • Long-term: When TypeScript 7.x ships a stable programmatic API, we'll evaluate a rewrite of the compiler integration.

 

Completely Independent but also Happening Now

In UI5 1.90.x times, years ago, when the new improved TypeScript support using ES modules (instead of the globals used until then), we called the new type packages @sapui5/ts-types-esm to differentiate them from the prior @sapui5/ts-types. Soon we renamed the new packages to.@sapui5/types for simplicity, but kept releasing the ts-types-esm variants, marked as deprecated. Now we finally stop shipping the variant with the longer name, so in case you still use @sapui5/ts-types-esm or @openui5/ts-types-esm, replace with the identical @sapui5/typesor @openui5/types.

 

All in all, What Should You Do?

Staying on TypeScript 5.x? Switch from @types/openui5 to @openui5/types when you get a chance. Everything else stays the same.

Upgrading to TypeScript 6? Update your tsconfig (module resolution, types field) as part of the general TypeScript 6 update procedure, switch to @openui5/types if you haven't yet, and everything else just works.

Eyeing TypeScript 7? The types will work from day one. For tooling (like@ui5/ts-interface-generator) — not just ours, but across the entire TypeScript ecosystem — the community needs time to adapt to the new architecture. We're watching the developments closely.

Still using @...ui5/ts-types-esm? Switch to @...ui5/types.

entry.title: Re: how to use "teams" within plant maintenance work centers when mulitple work centers bu
entry.content: Hi @abinash_routray Many thanks for your response. Sorry for my delay in answering your questions, I am on holiday. We are using Team Category SMNT_TEAM to create a team for Plant Maintenance - so all the relevant "jobs" are shown in the Perform Jobs app. There is no workflow associated with these teams. I hope this makes sense. Best regards Adrian

entry.title: Re: How to find authorization roles with an object and specific field values tied together?
entry.content: In that case I advice you to look at the reports in SUIM for "users by authorization values" and "roles by authorization values".
